title: The Claude C Compiler: What It Reveals About the Future of Software
description: Anthropic's Nicholas Carlini built a C compiler using parallel Claude instances. Chris Lattner (LLVM creator) reviewed it, noting it looks like a 'competent textbook implementation' but highlights that AI systems excel at assembling known techniques while struggling with open-ended generalization for production systems. Raises deep questions about AI and IP in open source.
author: Simon Willison (covering Nicholas Carlini and Chris Lattner)

title: Raspberry Pi Stock Surges Due to OpenClaw Excitement
description: Raspberry Pi stock spiked 30-42% in two days amid excitement about OpenClaw, a viral AI personal assistant that users are running on Raspberry Pi hardware. CEO Eben Upton also purchased stock, halting a months-long slide.
author: Simon Willison

title: Andrej Karpathy on 'Claws' as the Next Layer of the AI Stack
description: Andrej Karpathy tweeted about buying a Mac Mini to experiment with OpenClaw, defining 'Claws' as a new layer on top of LLM agents that handle orchestration, scheduling, context, tool calls, and persistence. He mentions NanoClaw (4000 lines of code) and other variants like nanobot, zeroclaw, ironclaw, picoclaw.
author: Simon Willison (covering Andrej Karpathy)

title: Google Gemini 3.1 Pro Released - Achieves 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2
description: Google released Gemini 3.1 Pro on February 20, 2026, ranking first across 12 tests. Achieved 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2 benchmark (double previous generation's score). Half the price of Claude Opus 4.6 at $2/$12 per million tokens.
author: Google

title: Taalas Serves Llama 3.1 8B at 17,000 Tokens/Second
description: Canadian hardware startup Taalas announced a custom hardware implementation of Llama 3.1 8B running at 17,000 tokens/second - so fast it looks like a screenshot. They describe their 'Silicon Llama' as 'aggressively quantized, combining 3-bit and 6-bit parameters.'
author: Simon Willison

title: ggml.ai Joins Hugging Face to Ensure Long-term Progress of Local AI
description: Georgi Gerganov's ggml.ai (creator of llama.cpp) is joining Hugging Face. This is significant because llama.cpp kicked off the local model movement in March 2023. They plan to integrate GGML with Transformers library and improve packaging and UX for local models.
author: Simon Willison

title: Prompt Caching Makes Long-Running Agent Products Feasible
description: Thariq Shihipar from Anthropic explains that Claude Code builds its entire harness around prompt caching. High cache hit rates decrease costs and enable more generous rate limits. They run alerts on cache hit rates and declare SEVs if they're too low.
author: Simon Willison (covering Thariq Shihipar)

title: Gemini 3.1 Pro Released with Pelican SVG Benchmark
description: Google released Gemini 3.1 Pro at half the price of Claude Opus 4.6 ($2/$12 per million tokens). Simon tested it with his signature 'pelican riding a bicycle' SVG generation prompt. Notably slow at launch (took 104s for 'hi'). Google's Jeff Dean responded with a video of animated pelicans and other animals riding vehicles.
author: Simon Willison

title: SWE-bench February 2026 Leaderboard Update
description: SWE-bench released fresh benchmark results testing models against 500 verified coding problems. Claude 4.5 Opus (high reasoning) leads at 76.8%, followed by Gemini 3 Flash (75.8%) and MiniMax M2.5 (75.8%). Notable that Claude 4.5 beats 4.6, and Chinese models (MiniMax, GLM-5, Kimi K2.5, DeepSeek V3.2) comprise 40% of top 10.
author: Simon Willison

title: Coming Around to Type Hints After 25+ Years
description: Simon Willison reflects on finally preferring type hints and strong typing after resisting them for 25+ years. The shift is because coding agents now do the typing, making the benefits of explicit type definitions much more attractive without the iteration speed penalty.
author: Simon Willison

title: Paul Ford: The AI Disruption We've Been Waiting For Has Arrived
description: Paul Ford's NYT opinion piece describes the November moment when AI coding tools suddenly got much better. He shares examples of completing projects worth $25,000 and $350,000 for the price of Claude's $200/month plan. Captures the tension: 'All the people I love hate this stuff, and all the people I hate love it.'
author: Simon Willison (covering Paul Ford)

title: Martin Fowler on LLMs Eating Specialty Skills
description: Martin Fowler shares insights from Thoughtworks retreat: 'LLMs are eating specialty skills. There will be less use of specialist front-end and back-end developers as the LLM-driving skills become more important.' Questions whether this leads to greater recognition of Expert Generalists.
author: Simon Willison (covering Martin Fowler)

title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 4.6
description: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 4.6 with performance similar to Opus 4.5 but at Sonnet pricing ($3/$15 per million tokens vs $5/$25). Knowledge cutoff of August 2025. Simon tested with pelican SVG benchmark - Sonnet 4.6 apparently loves adding top hats to pelicans.
author: Simon Willison

title: OpenClaw: The AI That Actually Does Things
description: OpenClaw (formerly Clawdbot/Moltbot) by Peter Steinberger is a self-hostable AI execution engine with 200,000+ GitHub stars. Unlike chatbots, it can execute tasks autonomously with OS-level access. February 17, 2026 version released with Sonnet 4.6 integration, 1M context testing, and iOS share extension.
author: Multiple sources (compiled from search results)

title: Peter Steinberger Joins OpenAI; OpenClaw Transitions to Foundation
description: OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger joined OpenAI on February 14, 2026. OpenClaw is transitioning to an independent foundation. The project has sparked Mac mini buying frenzy and derivatives like ZeroClaw (99% lower memory usage).
author: Multiple sources (compiled from search results)

title: Moonshot AI Launches Kimi Claw - Cloud-Based Zero-Deployment OpenClaw
description: Moonshot AI (月之暗面) launched Kimi Claw during Chinese New Year 2026 - a browser-based, zero-deployment version of OpenClaw powered by Kimi K2.5 model. Eliminates need for server setup or API configuration. Company raised $700M from Alibaba and Tencent, valuation doubled to exceed $10B.
author: Multiple sources (compiled from search results)

title: LLM Code Reviewers Are Harder to Fool Than Vulnerability Patterns Succeed
description: ArXiv paper published February 18, 2026 examining LLM code review capabilities. Study shows that while adversarial comments can confuse LLM reviewers, they're more robust than expected at detecting actual vulnerability patterns. Important research for understanding AI-assisted code review reliability.
author: Multiple researchers (arXiv:2602.16741)

title: Two Minute Papers: Google Gemini 3.1 Pro Review Video
description: Two Minute Papers released a video review of Google Gemini 3.1 Pro that gained over 1 million views within 1 hour. Video demonstrates the model's capabilities, compares it against GPT-5.2, and covers three major scenarios where it outperformed competitors. Continues channel's mission of making AI concepts accessible without complex academic terminology.
author: Two Minute Papers (Károly Zsolnai-Fehér)

title: AI Engineer Handbook: Comprehensive Resource Curated for 2026
description: GitHub repository 'AI Engineer Handbook' published February 17, 2026, compiling essential resources for AI engineers. Recommends top educational creators including 3Blue1Brown (6M+ followers), Yannic Kilcher (250K+), Two Minute Papers (1.5M+), and AI Explained (500K+). Represents curated knowledge map for AI engineering careers in 2026.
author: DataExpert-io (GitHub)
